title: Snow White
lines: A long time ago, in a far-off land, there lived a queen who had a daughter with skin as white as snow, lips as red as blood, and hair as black as ebony.
The queen died, and the king married another woman who was very beautiful but also very vain.
She had a magic mirror that she consulted every day, asking 'Mirror, mirror on the wall, who is the fairest of them all?'
The mirror always answered that the queen was the fairest.
As Snow White grew up, she became more and more beautiful.
One day when the queen asked the mirror her usual question, it answered that Snow White was now the fairest in the land.
The queen was furious with jealousy.
She ordered a hunter to take Snow White into the forest and kill her.
But the hunter took pity on the beautiful girl and let her go.
Poor Snow White fled through the forest, frightened and alone.
After wandering for many hours, she came to a little cottage.
It was so neat and clean, though everything was very small.
She ate a little food from each plate on the table and then went upstairs to sleep.
She found seven little beds and lay down in one.
When the owners returned, she woke to see seven dwarfs looking at her in amazement.
Snow White explained her sad story, and the kind dwarfs agreed to let her stay.
She kept house for them and was very happy.
But the queen discovered that Snow White was still alive.
She dressed herself as an old woman and went to the cottage.
She had a poisoned apple that would put Snow White into a death-like sleep.
Snow White, not knowing the danger, took a bite of the apple and fell down as if dead.
The dwarfs, finding her unconscious, were grief-stricken and placed her in a crystal coffin.
A prince came riding through the forest and saw the beautiful girl in the coffin.
He knelt and kissed her gently, and at that very moment, the spell was broken.
Snow White opened her eyes and sat up, alive and well.
She married the prince and went away to his kingdom to live happily ever after.
